bugs.title: Gemini primary model 404 — gemini-3-flash not found
bugs.description: The primary AI provider (Gemini) fails with 404 for model 'gemini-3-flash' on every request. All responses fall through to the Claude fallback. This caused one complete message failure when Claude was also rate-limited (429). The error message indicates the model name 'gemini-3-flash' is incorrect or not available in the toqui-staging project.
bugs.expected: Primary Gemini model should be available and respond successfully
bugs.actual: Every request returns 404 from Gemini: 'Publisher Model projects/toqui-staging/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/gemini-3-flash was not found'

bugs.title: Shared trip view returns empty JSON
bugs.description: GET /shared/{token} returns '{}' instead of trip data. The share token was generated successfully, but the public view endpoint returns no content.
bugs.expected: Shared trip view should return trip title, description, itinerary, etc.

bugs.title: itineraryUpdate stream event contains 0 items
bugs.description: When create_itinerary_items tool creates 28 items successfully (confirmed by toolResult with created_count:28), the itineraryUpdate stream event sent to the frontend contains 0 items. The items are persisted in the database correctly (verified via GetItinerary), but the real-time stream event is empty.
bugs.expected: itineraryUpdate event should contain the created items for frontend real-time update
bugs.actual: itineraryUpdate event has items:[] despite 28 items being created

ux_issues.description: First SendMessage request for full 3-week itinerary timed out (1m30s). Had to retry with a smaller scope (8 days instead of 21). Large itinerary generation may need longer timeouts or chunked generation.
ux_issues.suggestion: Increase turn timeout for itinerary generation or automatically chunk large requests

ux_issues.description: Companion mode response for 'what can I eat right now for under 100 rupees' was very brief (318 chars) and didn't list specific dishes or prices - just said 'go to Johari Bazaar'. Planning mode gave much richer food content for the same city.
ux_issues.suggestion: Companion mode should still provide 2-3 specific budget-appropriate food options with prices, especially when the user gives a specific budget constraint

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Itinerary Day 3 (Delhi) has Humayun's Tomb ($7) + Qutub Minar ($7) = $14 in entrance fees alone, exceeding the $10/day total budget. No budget warning was given for this day.

Itinerary Day 5 (Agra) has Taj Mahal ($16) + Mehtab Bagh ($3.50) + Fatehpur Sikri ($7) + bus ($1.20) = ~$28 in activities alone before food and accommodation. The AI later acknowledged this totals ~$31 but dismissed it as 'averaged across your whole trip it works out' rather than treating it as a serious budget violation requiring restructuring.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Day 5 itinerary + follow-up Agra budget question

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Itinerary Day 8 (Jaipur) suggests Chokhi Dhani at $10-12 for a single dinner, which equals or exceeds the entire daily budget. While an alternative was mentioned, leading with a budget-busting option undermines the $10/day constraint.

Multiple days in the itinerary have entrance fees alone exceeding $10. Days 2, 3, 5, 6, 7 all have paid attractions that when combined with accommodation and food would far exceed $10/day. The AI should have structured the itinerary with more free days and fewer paid attractions per day to stay within budget.

Taj Mahal budget was correctly flagged as exceeding daily budget (good), and workarounds were suggested (Mehtab Bagh for free view). However, the AI also suggested a composite ticket (Taj + Agra Fort) at 1350 INR without acknowledging this is still over budget.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Agra itinerary and follow-up budget discussion

tool_failures.input: India budget travel 2026 Delhi Agra Jaipur Varanasi $10 per day backpacker tips
tool_failures.error: Web search not configured in this environment — returned no results
turn timeout: AI provider did not respond within 1m30s

tool_failures.input: Agra free activities + Taj budget question (first attempt)
tool_failures.error: Both providers failed: Gemini 404 (gemini-3-flash not found) + Claude 429 rate limit

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: Trip creation was excellent — the AI immediately acknowledged the $10/day constraint and provided a realistic breakdown. Food, accommodation, and transport recommendations were all budget-appropriate and culturally accurate (vegetarian awareness, sleeper class trains, Paharganj dorms). However, budget enforcement in the itinerary was weak: multiple days had entrance fees alone exceeding the daily budget with no warning. The AI treated $10/day more as a vague guideline than a hard constraint. Companion mode was functional but too brief. The Gemini model 404 forced all requests through Claude fallback, causing one complete failure due to rate limiting.
